assumptions: The intragroup dividend declaration has created a recognised intragroup monetary receivable/payable exposure in the consolidated financial statements.
The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised intragroup monetary receivable that gives rise to foreign exchange gains or losses not fully eliminated on consolidation falls within the recognised-item hedge family in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Foreign currency risk within hedge accounting is also addressed through the cash flow hedge model, which is a canonical peer IFRS hedge-accounting model relevant to foreign-currency exposures.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS separately identifies hedges of foreign currency risk arising from a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct hedge-accounting model.
